steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank completes the subject noun phrase. The names "Laura Gee and Muriel Niederle" follow "economists" as an appositive — a phrase that identifies which economists the sentence is about. The question is whether and where commas belong.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "economists Laura Gee and Muriel Niederle,": adds a trailing comma after the appositive, incorrectly cutting it off from the rest of the predicate. ✗
  • "economists Laura Gee and Muriel Niederle": no commas anywhere. The appositive is essential (it identifies which economists), so no commas should set it off. ✓
  • "economists Laura Gee, and Muriel Niederle,": places a comma between the two names as if separating list items and adds a trailing comma, breaking the appositive apart. ✗
  • "economists, Laura Gee and Muriel Niederle,": wraps the appositive in commas as though it were nonessential, but the names are essential here — they identify the specific subject. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
